passage.text:
Raising the Mary Rose
How a sixteenth-century warship was recovered from the seabed
On 19 July 1545, English and French fleets were engaged in a sea battle off the coast of southern England in the area of water called the Solent, between Portsmouth and the Isle of Wight. Among the English vessels was a warship by the name of Mary Rose. Built in Portsmouth some 35 years earlier, she had had a long and successful fighting career, and was a favourite of King Henry VIII. Accounts of what happened to the ship vary: while witnesses agree that she was not hit by the French, some maintain that she was outdated, overladen and sailing too low in the water, others that she was mishandled by undisciplined crew. What is undisputed, however, is that the Mary Rose sank into the Solent that day, taking at least 500 men with her. After the battle, attempts were made to recover the ship, but these failed.
The Mary Rose came to rest on the seabed, lying on her starboard (right) side at an angle of approximately 60 degrees. The hull (the body of the ship) acted as a trap for the sand and mud carried by Solent currents. As a result, the starboard side filled rapidly, leaving the exposed port (left) side to be eroded by marine organisms and mechanical degradation. Because of the way the ship sank, nearly all of the starboard half survived intact. During the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries, the entire site became covered with a layer of hard grey clay, which minimised further erosion.
Then, on 16 June 1836, some fishermen in the Solent found that their equipment was caught on an underwater obstruction, which turned out to be the Mary Rose. Diver John Deane happened to be exploring another sunken ship nearby, and the fishermen approached him, asking him to free their gear. Deane dived down, and found the equipment caught on a timber protruding slightly from the seabed. Exploring further, he uncovered several other timbers and a bronze gun. Deane continued diving on the site intermittently until 1840, recovering several more guns, two bows, various timbers, part of a pump and various other small finds.
The Mary Rose then faded into obscurity for another hundred years. But in 1965, military historian and amateur diver Alexander McKee, in conjunction with the British Sub-Aqua Club, initiated a project called ‘Solent Ships’. While on paper this was a plan to examine a number of known wrecks in the Solent, what McKee really hoped for was to find the Mary Rose. Ordinary search techniques proved unsatisfactory, so McKee entered into collaboration with Harold E. Edgerton, professor of electrical engineering at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. In 1967, Edgerton’s side-scan sonar systems revealed a large, unusually shaped object, which McKee believed was the Mary Rose.
Further excavations revealed stray pieces of timber and an iron gun. But the climax to the operation came when, on 5 May 1971, part of the ship’s frame was uncovered. McKee and his team now knew for certain that they had found the wreck, but were as yet unaware that it also housed a treasure trove of beautifully preserved artefacts. Interest ^ in the project grew, and in 1979, The Mary Rose Trust was formed, with Prince Charles as its President and Dr Margaret Rule its Archaeological Director. The decision whether or not to salvage the wreck was not an easy one, although an excavation in 1978 had shown that it might be possible to raise the hull. While the original aim was to raise the hull if at all feasible, the operation was not given the go-ahead until January 1982, when all the necessary information was available.
An important factor in trying to salvage the Mary Rose was that the remaining hull was an open shell. This led to an important decision being taken: namely to carry out the lifting operation in three very distinct stages. The hull was attached to a lifting frame via a network of bolts and lifting wires. The problem of the hull being sucked back downwards into the mud was overcome by using 12 hydraulic jacks. These raised it a few centimetres over a period of several days, as the lifting frame rose slowly up its four legs. It was only when the hull was hanging freely from the lifting frame, clear of the seabed and the suction effect of the surrounding mud, that the salvage operation progressed to the second stage. In this stage, the lifting frame was fixed to a hook attached to a crane, and the hull was lifted completely clear of the seabed and transferred underwater into the lifting cradle. This required precise positioning to locate the legs into the stabbing guides’ of the lifting cradle. The lifting cradle was designed to fit the hull using archaeological survey drawings, and was fitted with air bags to provide additional cushioning for the hull’s delicate timber framework. The third and final stage was to lift the entire structure into the air, by which time the hull was also supported from below. Finally, on 11 October 1982, millions of people around the world held their breath as the timber skeleton of the Mary Rose was lifted clear of the water, ready to be returned home to Portsmouth.

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 1–4 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this

questions.prompt: There is some doubt about what caused the Mary Rose to sink.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “some doubt” (sự nghi ngờ/không chắc chắn), “what caused”, “Mary Rose to sink” (nguyên nhân chìm)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “Accounts of what happened to the ship vary: while witnesses agree that she was not hit by the French, some maintain that she was outdated, overladen and sailing too low in the water, others that she was mishandled by undisciplined crew”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “Accounts... vary” (các lời kể/báo cáo khác nhau) và việc người thì cho rằng tàu quá tải (overladen), người lại bảo do thủy thủ đoàn thiếu kỷ luật (mishandled by undisciplined crew) chứng tỏ nguyên nhân chính xác khiến tàu chìm vẫn còn những sự nghi ngờ, chưa đồng nhất.

=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: The Mary Rose was the only ship to sink in the battle of 19 July 1545.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “the only ship to sink” (con tàu duy nhất bị chìm), “battle of 19 July 1545”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Toàn bộ bài đọc (hoặc các đoạn kể về trận chiến ngày 19/07/1545).

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ tập trung kể về sự kiện tàu Mary Rose bị chìm vào ngày này, hoàn toàn không cung cấp bất kỳ thông tin nào để xác định xem nó có phải là con tàu "duy nhất" bị chìm trong trận chiến đó hay không.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Most of one side of the Mary Rose lay undamaged under the sea.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “King Henry VIII”, “captain”, “to blame” (đổ lỗi/khiển trách), “sinking”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1 hoặc Đoạn 2 (Phần nói về phản ứng của nhà vua): Trong bài đọc gốc có đoạn: “Witnesses agree that she was not hit by the French... King Henry VIII himself watched the disaster unfold from Southsea Castle, but there is no record of his personal opinion regarding who was responsible.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ nói rằng Vua Henry VIII đã tự mình chứng kiến thảm họa từ lâu đài Southsea, nhưng không có tài liệu nào ghi lại ý kiến cá nhân của ông về việc ai là người phải chịu trách nhiệm (no record of his personal opinion regarding who was responsible). Do đó, việc ông có đổ lỗi cho vị thuyền trưởng hay không là không thể xác định.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Alexander McKee knew that the wreck would contain many valuable historical objects.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “completely destroyed” (bị phá hủy hoàn toàn), “hit the seabed” (chạm đáy biển)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2 hoặc Đoạn 3 (Phần mô tả tình trạng xác tàu dưới biển): “The ship then sank into the mud, which preserved much of the hull... half of the Mary Rose lay survived, intact and filled with clay.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ ra rằng con tàu chìm xuống bùn và lớp bùn này đã bảo tồn phần lớn thân tàu (preserved much of the hull), một nửa con tàu vẫn sống sót và còn nguyên vẹn (lay survived, intact). Vì vậy, thông tin tàu bị "phá hủy hoàn toàn" (completely destroyed) là sai.

=> Đáp án: FALSE

questionGroups.instruction:
Match each statement with the correct option, A–G.
Write the correct letter in boxes 5–8 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: A search for the Mary Rose was launched.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“A search”, “launched” (được khởi động/bắt đầu)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: “But in 1965, military historian and amateur diver Alexander McKee... initiated a project called ‘Solent Ships’. While on paper this was a plan to examine a number of known wrecks... what McKee really hoped for was to find the Mary Rose”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “initiated a project” đồng nghĩa với “launched”. Việc Alexander McKee khởi động dự án này với mục đích thực sự là tìm kiếm tàu Mary Rose (find the Mary Rose) diễn ra vào năm 1965 (tương ứng với ký tự C trong danh sách đáp án của bạn).

=> Đáp án: C

questions.prompt: One person's exploration of the Mary Rose site stopped.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“one person’s exploration”, “stopped” (bị dừng lại)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: “Deane continued diving on the site intermittently until 1840, recovering several more guns, two bows... and various other small finds.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Trích dẫn chỉ ra chuyên gia lặn Deane đã tiếp tục lặn ở khu vực này một cách ngắt quãng cho đến năm (until) 1840. Từ "cho đến năm 1840" đồng nghĩa với việc hành trình khám phá của ông đã dừng lại vào mốc thời gian này (tương ứng với ký tự B trong danh sách đáp án của bạn).

=> Đáp án: B

questions.prompt: It was agreed that the hull of the Mary Rose should be raised.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “agreed” (được đồng ý/chấp thuận), “hull”, “should be raised” (nên được nâng lên/trục vớt)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn cuối (phần quyết định trục vớt): “The operation was not given the go-ahead until January 1982, when all the information was available to write a success-forecast for the project.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “given the go-ahead” (được bật đèn xanh/cho phép thực hiện) đồng nghĩa với “agreed”. Sự kiện này chính thức được thông qua vào tháng 1 năm 1982 (tương ứng với ký tự G).

=> Đáp án: G

questions.prompt: The site of the Mary Rose was found by chance.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “site”, “found by chance” (tìm thấy tình cờ/ngẫu nhiên)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: “Then, on 16 June 1836, some fishermen in the Solent found that their equipment was caught on an underwater obstruction, which turned out to be the Mary Rose.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Việc một số người đánh cá bị vướng lưới/dụng cụ vào một chướng ngại vật dưới nước, sau đó mới hóa ra đó chính là tàu Mary Rose chứng tỏ vị trí xác tàu được phát hiện hoàn toàn ngẫu nhiên và tình cờ. Sự kiện này diễn ra vào ngày 16 tháng 6 năm 1836 (tương ứng với ký tự A).

=> Đáp án: A

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: 9. _________attached to hull by wires
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“attached to hull”, “by wires” (bằng các sợi dây)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “The hull was attached to a lifting frame via a network of bolts and lifting wires”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đề bài tìm danh từ chỉ vật thể được gắn vào thân tàu (hull) thông qua hệ thống dây cáp nâng (lifting wires). Trong cấu trúc câu của bài đọc, vật thể đó chính là lifting frame (khung nâng).

=> Đáp án: a lifting frame

questions.prompt: 10. _________  to prevent hull being sucked into mud
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “prevent”, “hull being sucked into mud” (ngăn thân tàu bị hút vào bùn)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “The problem of the hull being sucked back downwards into the mud was overcome by using 12 hydraulic jacks.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “prevent... being sucked into” đồng nghĩa với cấu trúc giải quyết vấn đề “problem of... being sucked back downwards into... was overcome by”. Công cụ được sử dụng để xử lý việc này là hydraulic jacks (kích thủy lực).

=> Đáp án: hydraulic jacks

questions.prompt: legs are placed into 11. _________
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Legs”, “placed into” (được đặt/định vị vào)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “This required precise positioning to locate the legs into the ‘stabbing guides’ of the lifting cradle”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “placed into” đồng nghĩa với cụm “locate... into”. Danh từ chỉ nơi các chân cấu trúc (legs) được đưa vào chính là cụm từ nằm trong dấu ngoặc kép của bài đọc: stabbing guides.

=> Đáp án: stabbing guides

questions.prompt: hull is lowered into  12. _________
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Hull”, “lowered into” (được hạ/chuyển vào)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “...and the hull was lifted completely clear of the seabed and transferred underwater into the lifting cradle”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “lowered into” (hạ xuống/đặt vào vị trí thấp hơn bên dưới dòng nước) đồng nghĩa với hành động “transferred underwater into”. Nơi tiếp nhận thân tàu lúc này chính là chiếc lifting cradle (nôi nâng).

=> Đáp án: lifting cradle

questions.prompt: 13. _________ used as extra protection for the hull
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “used as”, “extra protection” (sự bảo vệ/giảm chấn bổ sung)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “...and was fitted with air bags to provide additional cushioning for the hull’s delicate timber framework.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “extra protection” đồng nghĩa với “additional cushioning” (lớp đệm bổ sung bảo vệ khung gỗ nhạy cảm). Vật mút/đệm được trang bị để thực hiện chức năng này là các air bags (túi khí).

=> Đáp án: air bags

questions.prompt: Gap (14)

questionGroups.note:
• [9] attached to hull by wires
 • [10] to prevent hull being sucked into mud 
 • legs are placed into [11] 
 • hull is lowered into [12] 
 • [13] used as extra protection for the hull

parts.passageTitle: What Destroyed the Civilisation of Easter Island?

passage.text:
What destroyed the civilisation of Easter Island?
What destroyed the civilisation of Easter Island?
A.
Easter Island, or Rapu Nui as it is known locally, is home to several hundred ancient human statues – the moai. After this remote Pacific island was settled by the Polynesians, it remained isolated for centuries. All the energy and resources that went into the moai – some of which are ten metres tall and weigh over 7,000 kilos – came from the island itself. Yet when Dutch explorers landed in 1722, they met a Stone Age culture. The moai were carved with stone tools, then transported for many kilometres, without the use of animals or wheels, to massive stone platforms. The identity of the moai builders was in doubt until well into the twentieth century. Thor Heyerdahl, the Norwegian ethnographer and adventurer, thought the statues had been created by pre-Inca peoples from Peru. Bestselling Swiss author Erich von Daniken believed they were built by stranded extraterrestrials. Modern science – linguistic, archaeological and genetic evidence – has definitively proved the moai builders were Polynesians, but not how they moved their creations. Local folklore maintains that the statues walked, while researchers have tended to assume the ancestors dragged the statues somehow, using ropes and logs.
B.
When the Europeans arrived, Rapa Nui was grassland, with only a few scrawny trees. In the 1970s and 1980s, though, researchers found pollen preserved in lake sediments, which proved the island had been covered in lush palm forests for thousands of years. Only after the Polynesians arrived did those forests disappear. US scientist Jared Diamond believes that the Rapanui people – descendants of Polynesian settlers – wrecked their own environment. They had unfortunately settled on an extremely fragile island – dry, cool, and too remote to be properly fertilised by windblown volcanic ash. When the islanders cleared the forests for firewood and farming, the forests didn’t grow back. As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing, they ate birds. Soil erosion decreased their crop yields. Before Europeans arrived, the Rapanui had descended into civil war and cannibalism, he maintains. The collapse of their isolated civilisation, Diamond writes, is a ’worst-case scenario for what may lie ahead of us in our own future’.
C.
The moai, he thinks, accelerated the self-destruction. Diamond interprets them as power displays by rival chieftains who, trapped on a remote little island, lacked other ways of asserting their dominance. They competed by building ever bigger figures. Diamond thinks they laid the moai on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails, but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people. To feed the people, even more land had to be cleared. When the wood was gone and civil war began, the islanders began toppling the moai. By the nineteenth century none were standing.
D.
Archaeologists Terry Hunt of the University of Hawaii and Carl Lipo of California State University agree that Easter Island lost its lush forests and that it was an ‘ecological catastrophe’ – but they believe the islanders themselves weren’t to blame. And the moai certainly weren’t. Archaeological excavations indicate that the Rapanui went to heroic efforts to protect the resources of their wind-lashed, infertile fields. They built thousands of circular stone windbreaks and gardened inside them, and used broken volcanic rocks to keep the soil moist. In short, Hunt and Lipo argue, the prehistoric Rapanui were pioneers of sustainable farming.
E.
Hunt and Lipo contend that moai-building was an activity that helped keep the peace between islanders. They also believe that moving the moai required few people and no wood, because they were walked upright. On that issue, Hunt and Lipo say, archaeological evidence backs up Rapanui folklore. Recent experiments indicate that as few as 18 people could, with three strong ropes and a bit of practice, easily manoeuvre a 1,000 kg moai replica a few hundred metres. The figures’ fat bellies tilted them forward, and a D-shaped base allowed handlers to roll and rock them side to side.
F.
Moreover, Hunt and Lipo are convinced that the settlers were not wholly responsible for the loss of the island’s trees. Archaeological finds of nuts from the extinct Easter Island palm show tiny grooves, made by the teeth of Polynesian rats. The rats arrived along with the settlers, and in just a few years, Hunt and Lipo calculate, they would have overrun the island. They would have prevented the reseeding of the slow-growing palm trees and thereby doomed Rapa Nui’s forest, even without the settlers’ campaign of deforestation. No doubt the rats ate birds’ eggs too. Hunt and Lipo also see no evidence that Rapanui civilisation collapsed when the palm forest did. They think its population grew rapidly and then remained more or less stable until the arrival of the Europeans, who introduced deadly diseases to which islanders had no immunity. Then in the nineteenth century slave traders decimated the population, which shrivelled to 111 people by 1877.
G.
Hunt and Lipo’s vision, therefore, is one of an island populated by peaceful and ingenious moai builders and careful stewards of the land, rather than by reckless destroyers ruining their own environment and society. ‘Rather than a case of abject failure, Rapu Nui is an unlikely story of success’, they claim. Whichever is the case, there are surely some valuable lessons which the world at large can learn from the story of Rapa Nui.

questionGroups.instruction:
Reading Passage has paragraphs A–F. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–ix, in boxes 14–20 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: Paragraph A
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph A”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn A: “Modern science - linguistic, archaeological and genetic evidence - has definitively proved the moai builders were Polynesians...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ ra rằng các ngành khoa học hiện đại đã chứng minh một cách chắc chắn (definitively proved) danh tính những người xây tượng là người Polynesia. Điều này hoàn toàn khớp với heading ii: “An undisputed answer to a question about the moai” (Một câu trả lời không còn bị tranh cãi cho câu hỏi về các bức tượng moai).

=> Đáp án: ii

questions.prompt: Paragraph B
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph B”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing, they ate birds. Soil erosion decreased their crop yields.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi nguồn tài nguyên cây cối cạn kiệt, người dân không thể đóng thuyền đi đánh cá mà phải ăn thịt chim, đồng thời năng suất cây trồng bị giảm sút. Các thông tin này mô tả việc nguồn thực phẩm bị thu hẹp lại, khớp với heading ix: “Diminishing food resources” (Nguồn tài nguyên lương thực giảm dần).

=> Đáp án: ix

questions.prompt: Paragraph C
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph C”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “The moai, he thinks, accelerated the self-destruction... Diamond thinks they laid the moai on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails, but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people. To feed the people, even more land had to be cleared.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Theo giả thuyết của Diamond, việc chế tạo và vận chuyển các bức tượng moai đã đẩy nhanh quá trình tự hủy diệt vì nó tiêu tốn quá nhiều gỗ và đất đai. Việc này khớp với heading viii: “How the statues made a situation worse” (Cách các bức tượng làm cho tình hình tồi tệ hơn).

=> Đáp án: viii

questions.prompt: Paragraph D
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph D”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “They built thousands of circular stone windbreaks and gardened inside them, and used broken volcanic rocks to keep the soil moist. In short, Hunt and Lipo argue, the prehistoric Rapanui were pioneers of sustainable farming.”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn mô tả các biện pháp nông nghiệp sáng tạo của người cổ đại như xây tường đá chắn gió, dùng đá núi lửa để giữ ẩm cho đất để canh tác bền vững. Thông tin này khớp với heading i: “Evidence of innovative environment management practices” (Bằng chứng về các hoạt động quản lý môi trường đổi mới/sáng tạo).

=> Đáp án: i

questions.prompt: Paragraph E
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph E”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn E: “They also believe that moving the moai required few people and no wood, because they were walked upright. On that issue, Hunt and Lipo say, archaeological evidence backs up Rapanui folklore.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Hunt và Lipo đưa ra giả thuyết rằng các bức tượng được di chuyển ở tư thế đứng thẳng. Nghiên cứu thực nghiệm này đã củng cố/ủng hộ cho câu chuyện dân gian địa phương (backs up Rapanui folklore) rằng các bức tượng biết đi. Điều này khớp với heading iv: “A theory which supports a local belief” (Một lý thuyết ủng hộ niềm tin của địa phương).

=> Đáp án: iv

questions.prompt: Paragraph F
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph F”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn F: “The rats arrived along with the settlers, and in just a few years... they would have overrun the island. They would have prevented the reseeding of the slow-growing palm trees and thereby doomed Rapa Nui’s forest...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ ra sự tàn phá rừng cọ phần lớn là do loài chuột Polynesia ăn hạt cây khiến rừng không thể tái sinh, cộng thêm các dịch bệnh chết người do người châu Âu mang tới. Đây đều là những yếu tố phá hủy nằm ngoài tầm kiểm soát của cư dân bản địa, khớp với heading vii: “Destruction outside the inhabitants’ control” (Sự phá hủy ngoài tầm kiểm soát của cư dân).

=> Đáp án: vii

questions.prompt: Paragraph G
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph G”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G: “Hunt and Lipo’s vision, therefore, is one of an island populated by peaceful and ingenious moai builders... rather than by reckless destroyers ruining their own environment and society.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn này đúc kết và đối chiếu lại hai góc nhìn hoàn toàn trái ngược nhau về người Rapanui: một bên là những người xây dựng hòa bình, khéo léo (Hunt & Lipo) và một bên là những kẻ hủy hoại môi trường liều lĩnh (Diamond). Điều này khớp với heading vi: “Two opposing views about the Rapanui people” (Hai quan điểm trái ngược nhau về người Rapanui).

=> Đáp án: vi

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: Diamond believes that the Polynesian settlers destroyed its forests, cutting down its trees for fuel and clearing land for ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “destroyed its forests”, “cutting down trees for fuel”, “clearing land for”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “When the islanders cleared the forests for firewood and farming, the forests didn’t grow back.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “cleared the forests for firewood” đồng nghĩa với “cutting down its trees for fuel”. Mục đích tiếp theo của việc dọn đất (clearing land for) đi liền sau từ firewood trong bài đọc chính là danh từ farming.

=> Đáp án: farming

questions.prompt: When the islanders were no longer able to build the ___________ they needed to go fishing, they began using the island's birds as food.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “no longer able to build”, “needed to go fishing”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “no longer able to build” đồng nghĩa với “could no longer construct”. Vật thể họ cần chế tạo để phục vụ mục đích đánh cá (for fishing) chính là các chiếc thuyền gỗ — canoes.

=> Đáp án: canoes

questions.prompt: They began using the island's ___________ as a food source.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “began using”, “as a food source” (như một nguồn thức ăn)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “...and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing, they ate birds.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi không thể đi đánh cá ngoài khơi xa được nữa, họ bắt đầu chuyển sang ăn (ate) đối tượng trên đảo để làm nguồn thức ăn thay thế. Danh từ chỉ đối tượng đó là birds.

=> Đáp án: birds

questions.prompt: the methods of transporting the statues needed not only a great number of people, but also a great deal of __________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “methods of transporting”, “needed”, “great number of people”, “great deal of” (rất nhiều...)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “Diamond thinks they laid the moai on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails, but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “required both a lot of wood and a lot of people” đồng nghĩa với “needed not only a great number of people, but also a great deal of...”. Thành phần tài nguyên còn thiếu đi kèm với con người ở đây là wood.

=> Đáp án: wood

questionGroups.note: Diamond believes that the Polynesian settlers on Rapa Nui destroyed its forests, cutting down its trees for fuel and clearing land for [21] Twentieth-century discoveries of pollen prove that Rapu Nui had once been covered in palm forests, which had turned into grassland by the time the Europeans arrived on the island. When the islanders were no longer able to build the [22] they needed to go fishing, they began using the island’s [23] as a food source, according to Diamond. Diamond also claims that the moai were built to show the power of the island’s chieftains, and that the methods of transporting the statues needed not only a great number of people, but also a great deal of [24]

questions.prompt: On what points do Hunt and Lipo disagree with Diamond?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“Hunt and Lipo”, “disagree with Diamond” (không đồng ý/bất đồng ý kiến với Diamond)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Đoạn E và Đoạn G.
Bước 3: Phân tích: * Bất đồng thứ nhất nằm ở cách thức vận chuyển tượng: Diamond nghĩ rằng tượng được đặt trên xe trượt gỗ và kéo đi (laid on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails – Đoạn C), trong khi Hunt và Lipo tin rằng các bức tượng được di chuyển ở tư thế đứng thẳng bằng dây thừng (walked upright – Đoạn E) 
-> Khác biệt về how the moai were transported (Đáp án B).
Bất đồng thứ hai nằm ở tầm ảnh hưởng của các bức tượng đối với xã hội: Diamond cho rằng các bức tượng là màn phô diễn quyền lực của các tộc trưởng đối đầu và đẩy nhanh quá trình tự hủy diệt xã hội (accelerated the self-destruction – Đoạn C), trái lại Hunt và Lipo khẳng định việc xây tượng là hoạt động giúp duy trì hòa bình giữa các bộ tộc (activity that helped keep the peace – Đoạn E) -> Khác biệt về the impact of the moai on Rapanui society (Đáp án C).
=> Đáp án: B, C

options.label: A. the period when the moai were created
B. how the moai were transported
C. the impact of the moai on Rapanui society
D. how the moai were carved
E. the origins of the people who made the moai

questions.prompt: On what points do Hunt and Lipo disagree with Diamond?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“Hunt and Lipo”, “disagree with Diamond” (không đồng ý/bất đồng ý kiến với Diamond)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Đoạn E và Đoạn G.
Bước 3: Phân tích: * Bất đồng thứ nhất nằm ở cách thức vận chuyển tượng: Diamond nghĩ rằng tượng được đặt trên xe trượt gỗ và kéo đi (laid on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails – Đoạn C), trong khi Hunt và Lipo tin rằng các bức tượng được di chuyển ở tư thế đứng thẳng bằng dây thừng (walked upright – Đoạn E) 
-> Khác biệt về how the moai were transported (Đáp án B).
Bất đồng thứ hai nằm ở tầm ảnh hưởng của các bức tượng đối với xã hội: Diamond cho rằng các bức tượng là màn phô diễn quyền lực của các tộc trưởng đối đầu và đẩy nhanh quá trình tự hủy diệt xã hội (accelerated the self-destruction – Đoạn C), trái lại Hunt và Lipo khẳng định việc xây tượng là hoạt động giúp duy trì hòa bình giữa các bộ tộc (activity that helped keep the peace – Đoạn E) -> Khác biệt về the impact of the moai on Rapanui society (Đáp án C).
=> Đáp án: B, C

options.label: A. the period when the moai were created
B. how the moai were transported
C. the impact of the moai on Rapanui society
D. how the moai were carved
E. the origins of the people who made the moai

passage.text:
Neuroaesthetics
An emerging discipline called neuroaesthetics is seeking to bring scientific objectivity to the study of art, and has already given us a better understanding of many masterpieces. The blurred imagery of Impressionist paintings seems to stimulate the brain’s amygdala, for instance. Since the amygdala plays a crucial role in our feelings, that finding might explain why many people find these pieces so moving.
Could the same approach also shed light on abstract twentieth-century pieces, from Mondrian’s geometrical blocks of colour, to Pollock’s seemingly haphazard arrangements of splashed paint on canvas? Sceptics believe that people claim to like such works simply because they are famous. We certainly do have an inclination to follow the crowd. When asked to make simple perceptual decisions such as matching a shape to its rotated image, for example, people often choose a definitively wrong answer if they see others doing the same. It is easy to imagine that this mentality would have even more impact on a fuzzy concept like art appreciation, where there is no right or wrong answer.
Angelina Hawley-Dolan, of Boston College, Massachusetts, responded to this debate by asking volunteers to view pairs of paintings – either the creations of famous abstract artists or the doodles of infants, chimps and elephants. They then had to judge which they preferred. A third of the paintings were given no captions, while many were labelled incorrectly -volunteers might think they were viewing a chimp’s messy brushstrokes when they were actually seeing an acclaimed masterpiece. In each set of trials, volunteers generally preferred the work of renowned artists, even when they believed it was by an animal or a child. It seems that the viewer can sense the artist’s vision in paintings, even if they can’t explain why.
Robert Pepperell, an artist based at Cardiff University, creates ambiguous works that are neither entirely abstract nor clearly representational. In one study, Pepperell and his collaborators asked volunteers to decide how’powerful’they considered an artwork to be, and whether they saw anything familiar in the piece. The longer they took to answer these questions, the more highly they rated the piece under scrutiny, and the greater their neural activity. It would seem that the brain sees these images as puzzles, and the harder it is to decipher the meaning, the more rewarding is the moment of recognition.
And what about artists such as Mondrian, whose paintings consist exclusively of horizontal and vertical lines encasing blocks of colour? Mondrian’s works are deceptively simple, but eye-tracking studies confirm that they are meticulously composed, and that simpiy rotating a piece radically changes the way we view it. With the originals, volunteers’eyes tended to stay longer on certain places in the image, but with the altered versions they would flit across a piece more rapidly. As a result, the volunteers considered the altered versions less pleasurable when they later rated the work.
In a similar study, Oshin Vartanian of Toronto University asked volunteers to compare original paintings with ones which he had altered by moving objects around within the frame. He found that almost everyone preferred the original, whether it was a Van Gogh still life or an abstract by Miro. Vartanian also found that changing the composition of the paintings reduced activation in those brain areas linked with meaning and interpretation.
In another experiment, Alex Forsythe of the University of Liverpool analysed the visual intricacy of different pieces of art, and her results suggest that many artists use a key level of detail to please the brain. Too little and the work is boring, but too much results in a kind of ‘perceptual overload’, according to Forsythe. What’s more, appealing pieces both abstract and representational, show signs of ‘fractals’ – repeated motifs recurring in different scales, fractals are common throughout nature, for example in the shapes of mountain peaks or the branches of trees. It is possible that our visual system, which evolved in the great outdoors, finds it easier to process such patterns.
It is also intriguing that the brain appears to process movement when we see a handwritten letter, as if we are replaying the writer’s moment of creation. This has led some to wonder whether Pollock’s works feel so dynamic because the brain reconstructs the energetic actions the artist used as he painted. This may be down to our brain’s ‘mirror neurons’, which are known to mimic others’ actions. The hypothesis will need to be thoroughly tested, however. It might even be the case that we could use neuroaesthetic studies to understand the longevity of some pieces of artwork. While the fashions of the time might shape what is currently popular, works that are best adapted to our visual system may be the most likely to linger once the trends of previous generations have been forgotten.
It’s still early days for the field of neuroaesthetics – and these studies are probably only a taste of what is to come. It would, however, be foolish to reduce art appreciation to a set of scientific laws. We shouldn’t underestimate the importance of the style of a particular artist, their place in history and the artistic environment of their time. Abstract art offers both a challenge and the freedom to play with different interpretations. In some ways, it’s not so different to science, where we are constantly looking for systems and decoding meaning so that we can view and appreciate the world in a new way.

questions.prompt: In the second paragraph, the writer refers to a shape-matching test in order to illustrate
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “second paragraph”, “shape-matching test”, “illustrate”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 2: “We certainly do have an inclination to follow the crowd. When asked to make simple perceptual decisions such as matching a shape to its rotated image, for example, people often choose a definitively wrong answer if they see others doing the same.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Người viết đưa ra ví dụ về bài kiểm tra ghép hình dạng (matching a shape) để làm rõ cho luận điểm đứng ngay trước đó: Chúng ta có xu hướng đi theo đám đông (follow the crowd), tức là dễ bị tác động bởi ý kiến hay hành động của người khác.
=> Đáp án: C

options.label: A. the subjective nature of art appreciation.
B. the reliance of modern art on abstract forms.
C. our tendency to be influenced by the opinions of others.
D. a common problem encountered when processing visual data.

questions.prompt: Angelina Hawley-Dolan's findings indicate that people
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Angelina Hawley-Dolan’s findings”, “indicate”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 3: “In each set of trials, volunteers generally preferred the work of renowned artists, even when they believed it was by an animal or a child. It seems that the viewer can sense the artist's vision in paintings, even if they can't explain why.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Kết quả thí nghiệm cho thấy người xem vẫn luôn thích tranh của họa sĩ nổi tiếng hơn dù bị dán nhãn sai. Tác giả kết luận người xem có khả năng cảm nhận được tầm nhìn/ý đồ nghệ thuật (sense the artist's vision - tương đương với perceive the intention) ẩn chứa bên trong bức tranh.

=> Đáp án: D

options.label: A. mostly favour works of art which they know well.
B. hold fixed ideas about what makes a good work of art.
C. are often misled by their initial expectations of a work of art.
D. have the ability to perceive the intention behind works of art.

questions.prompt: Results of studies involving Robert Pepperell's pieces suggest that people
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Robert Pepperell”, “suggest”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 4: “It would seem that the brain sees these images as puzzles, and the harder it is to decipher the meaning, the more rewarding is the moment of recognition.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Nghiên cứu chỉ ra não bộ coi các bức tranh mơ hồ của Pepperell như một câu đố, và việc giải mã được ý nghĩa của nó càng khó khăn thì khoảnh khắc nhận ra (moment of recognition) lại càng mang lại cảm giác thỏa mãn, xứng đáng (rewarding – tương đương với satisfying).

=> Đáp án: B

options.label: A. can appreciate a painting without fully understanding it.
B. find it satisfying to work out what a painting represents.
C. vary widely in the time they spend looking at paintings.
D. generally prefer representational art to abstract art.

questions.prompt: What do the experiments described in the fifth paragraph suggest about the paintings of Mondrian?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “fifth paragraph”, “paintings of Mondrian”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 5: “Mondrian's works are deceptively simple, but eye-tracking studies confirm that they are meticulously composed...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Các tác phẩm của Mondrian có vẻ ngoài đơn giản một cách đánh lừa (deceptively simple), nhưng các nghiên cứu theo dõi mắt lại xác nhận rằng chúng thực ra được cấu trúc và sắp xếp vô cùng tỉ mỉ, cẩn thận (meticulously composed – đồng nghĩa với carefully put together).

=> Đáp án: A

options.label: A. They are more carefully put together than they appear.
B. They can be interpreted in a number of different ways.
C. They challenge our assumptions about shape and colour.
D. They are easier to appreciate than many other abstract works.

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–H, below.

questions.prompt: Neurological studies of the brain demonstrate the impact which Impressionist paintings have on our ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Neurological studies”, “Impressionist paintings”, “impact on our”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 1: “The blurred imagery of Impressionist paintings seems to stimulate the brain's amygdala, for instance. Since the amygdala plays a crucial role in our feelings, that finding might explain why many people find these pieces so moving.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tranh trường phái Ấn tượng kích thích hạch hạnh nhân – nơi đóng vai trò quan trọng trong việc quản lý cảm xúc (feelings). Trong danh sách từ cho sẵn, danh từ đồng nghĩa với feelings là emotions (đáp án C).

=> Đáp án: C

questions.prompt: Alex Forsythe believes many artists give their works the precise degree of ___________ which most appeals to the viewer's brain.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“Alex Forsythe”, “precise degree of” (mức độ chính xác của cái gì)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 7: “Alex Forsythe of the University of Liverpool analysed the visual intricacy of different pieces of art, and her results suggest that many artists use a key level of detail to please the brain.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Forsythe phân tích độ phức tạp về mặt thị giác (visual intricacy) và thấy các nghệ sĩ thường dùng một lượng chi tiết vừa đủ (key level of detail). Sự kết hợp giữa intricacy và detail tương đương với từ complexity (độ phức tạp – đáp án B).

=> Đáp án: B

questions.prompt: She also observes that pleasing works of art often contain certain repeated ___________ which occur frequently in the natural world.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “contain certain repeated”, “occur frequently in the natural world”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 7: “What's more, appealing pieces... show signs of 'fractals' - repeated motifs recurring in different scales, fractals are common throughout nature...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Các tác phẩm thu hút thường chứa các cấu trúc phân dạng (fractals), bản chất của chúng là các họa tiết lặp đi lặp lại (repeated motifs). Từ đồng nghĩa với motifs trong bảng từ cho sẵn là images (hình ảnh/họa tiết – đáp án H).

=> Đáp án: H

questionGroups.note: The discipline of neuroaesthetics aims to bring scientific objectivity to the study of art. Neurological studies of the brain, for example, demonstrate the impact which Impressionist paintings have on our [31] Alex Forsythe of the University of Liverpool believes many artists give their works the precise degree of [32] which most appeals to the viewer’s brain. She also observes that pleasing works of art often contain certain repeated [33]  which occur frequently in the natural world.

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 34–39 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.prompt: Forsythe's findings contradicted previous beliefs on the function of 'fractals' in art.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Forsythe’s findings”, “contradicted previous beliefs” (mâu thuẫn với niềm tin trước đây)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 7: “What's more, appealing pieces both abstract and representational, show signs of 'fractals' - repeated motifs recurring in different scales...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ đề cập đến việc Forsythe tìm ra dấu hiệu của cấu trúc hình học phân dạng (fractals) trong các tác phẩm nghệ thuật, hoàn toàn không có thông tin nào nói về việc phát hiện này có đi ngược hay mâu thuẫn với các quan điểm trước đây hay không.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

options.label: YES
NO
NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Certain ideas regarding the link between 'mirror neurons' and art appreciation require further verification.
questions.explanation:
Certain ideas regarding the link between ‘mirror neurons’ and art appreciation require further verification.

Bước 1: Keyword: “link”, “mirror neurons”, “art appreciation”, “require further verification” (cần xác minh/kiểm tra thêm)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 8: “This may be down to our brain's 'mirror neurons'... The hypothesis will need to be thoroughly tested, however.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả đưa ra giả thuyết mối liên hệ này có thể do tế bào thần kinh phản chiếu (mirror neurons), nhưng ngay sau đó khẳng định giả thuyết này sẽ cần phải được kiểm tra một cách kỹ lưỡng (need to be thoroughly tested). Cụm này đồng nghĩa với require further verification.

=> Đáp án: YES

options.label: YES
NO
NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: People's taste in paintings depends entirely on the current artistic trends of the period.
questions.explanation:
People’s taste in paintings depends entirely on the current artistic trends of the period.

Bước 1: Keyword: “taste”, “depends entirely on” (phụ thuộc hoàn toàn vào), “current artistic trends”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 8: “While the fashions of the time might shape what is currently popular, works that are best adapted to our visual system may be the most likely to linger once the trends of previous generations have been forgotten.”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: Xu hướng thời đại đúng là có định hình những gì đang phổ biến, nhưng những tác phẩm trường tồn lâu dài lại là những tác phẩm thích ứng tốt nhất với hệ thống thị giác tự nhiên của con người. Vì vậy, nói gu thẩm mỹ phụ thuộc "hoàn toàn" (entirely) vào xu hướng là sai.

=> Đáp án: NO

options.label: YES
NO
NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Scientists should seek to define the precise rules which govern people's reactions to works of art.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Scientists should seek”, “define the precise rules” (xác định các quy luật chính xác)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 9: “It would, however, be foolish to reduce art appreciation to a set of scientific laws. We shouldn't underestimate the importance of the style of a particular artist...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả nêu rõ quan điểm rằng thật là dại dột/ngớ ngẩn (foolish) nếu quy giản việc đánh giá nghệ thuật thành một tập hợp các quy luật khoa học cố định (reduce art appreciation to a set of scientific laws). Do đó, nhà khoa học không nên cố gắng tìm ra các quy tắc cứng nhắc này.

=> Đáp án: NO

options.label: YES
NO
NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Art appreciation should always involve taking into consideration the cultural context in which an artist worked.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“always involve”, “taking into consideration the cultural context” (xem xét bối cảnh văn hóa lịch sử)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 9: “We shouldn't underestimate the importance of the style of a particular artist, their place in history and the artistic environment of their time.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả khẳng định không được đánh giá thấp tầm quan trọng về phong cách, vị trí trong lịch sử cũng như môi trường nghệ thuật đương thời của người họa sĩ (artistic environment of their time - bối cảnh văn hóa xã hội). Điều này đồng nghĩa với việc ta luôn cần phải cân nhắc đến bối cảnh lịch sử/văn hóa của họ.

=> Đáp án: YES

options.label: YES
NO
NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: It is easier to find meaning in the field of science than in that of art.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “easier to find meaning”, “science than in that of art”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 9: “In some ways, it's not so different to science, where we are constantly looking for systems and decoding meaning so that we can view and appreciate the world in a new way.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả chỉ so sánh nghệ thuật trừu tượng và khoa học có điểm tương đồng là cả hai bên đều liên tục tìm kiếm hệ thống và giải mã ý nghĩa (decoding meaning). Bài viết hoàn toàn không đưa ra nhận định bên nào "dễ tìm thấy ý nghĩa hơn" (easier).

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

options.label: YES
NO
NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: What would be the most appropriate subtitle for the article?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “most appropriate subtitle” (tiêu đề phụ phù hợp nhất)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Xem xét nội dung bao quát toàn bài.

Bước 3: Phân tích: * Bài đọc tập trung giới thiệu về ngành thẩm mỹ thần kinh (neuroaesthetics) và các thí nghiệm chụp não, theo dõi mắt để phân tích phản ứng của con người trước các tác phẩm nghệ thuật trừu tượng (như tranh của Mondrian, Pollock, Miro...).

Đáp án A thể hiện trọn vẹn và chính xác nhất nội dung này: "Some scientific insights into how the brain responds to abstract art" (Một vài góc nhìn khoa học về cách bộ não phản ứng với nghệ thuật trừu tượng).

Các câu B, C, D đều bị sai hướng (B tập trung vào não của người họa sĩ, C tập trung so sánh tranh trừu tượng với tranh tả thực, D tập trung vào sự thay đổi ý kiến của công chúng).

=> Đáp án: A

options.label: A. Some scientific insights into how the brain responds to abstract art
B. Recent studies focusing on the neural activity of abstract artists
C. A comparison of the neurological bases of abstract and representational art
D. How brain research has altered public opinion about abstract art
